title: Double-encoded UTF-8 causes parser errors (mojibake)
description: Files containing Unicode characters (box-drawing, em-dash, etc.) that were saved by a tool interpreting UTF-8 bytes as Windows-1252 and re-encoding as UTF-8 produce double-encoded (mojibake) sequences. PowerShell parser cannot parse these files, producing errors like 'Unexpected token' or 'Missing closing }'. The signature byte pattern is C3 A2 E2 80 (UTF-8 re-encoding of the UTF-8 lead byte 0xE2). Example: U+2500 BOX DRAWINGS LIGHT HORIZONTAL (E2 94 80) becomes C3 A2 C2 94 C2 80 when double-encoded. This blocks module loading and script execution entirely.
detection_method: Byte-level scan for signature C3 A2 E2 80 in .ps1/.psm1 files. Alternatively: read file as UTF-8, encode as Windows-1252, re-decode as UTF-8 -- if the output differs from input, the file has double-encoding.
known_instances: PwShGUI-IntegrityCore.psm1: Parser error at line 44 'Missing closing }' due to control chars in double-encoded section headers
AssistedSASC.psm1: Parser error 'Unexpected token LAN Sharing' at line 2277 from double-encoded box-drawing chars
AVPN-Tracker.psm1, PwShGUI-ConvoVault.psm1, SASC-Adapters.psm1, UserProfileManager.psm1, H-Ai-Nikr-Agi.psm1, PipelineSteering.psm1
exception_message: ParserError: Unexpected token / Missing closing '}' in statement block or type definition
preventionRule:
1. Always save files as UTF-8 WITH BOM (SIN-P006) using a UTF-8-aware editor.
2. When using Set-Content or Out-File, always specify -Encoding UTF8.
3. Never use editors or tools that interpret UTF-8 as Windows-1252/Latin-1.
4. Add a CI/pre-commit check that scans for the double-encoding byte signature (C3 A2 E2 80).
5. If automated remediation tools rewrite files, verify encoding is preserved.
trusted_fix_notes: Applied round-trip decode to 8 files on 2026-04-04. All parsed cleanly after fix.
